settings.title: Meet the Trainees Behind the Desk
settings.excerpt: Our two newest trainee solicitors on what their first six months at the firm have actually been like.
settings.body:
People imagine a law firm as rows of senior partners. A lot of the careful work happens earlier in a career, with trainees who are learning the craft.

This year we welcomed two trainee solicitors, Marcus and Leila, who rotate through our property, commercial, and family teams over two years. We asked them what surprised them most.

What the first six months looked like

The rotations are designed so that neither of them leaves with a narrow view of the law. A few months in property gives way to commercial work, then family matters, and each move forces them to learn a new rhythm. It is demanding by design. We would rather they meet the breadth of the job early than discover it years in.

Across those first six months, a few experiences shaped them most.

  • Sitting in on client meetings from the very first week
  • Drafting documents that a partner then takes apart line by line
  • Watching a tense matter settle because someone listened well

The part no textbook teaches

The answer from both was how much of the job is listening. Before any letter is drafted, there is a client who needs to feel they have been heard and understood. That is the part no textbook teaches, and the part we care most about getting right.

Marcus said the biggest shift was realising that the law is rarely the hard bit. The hard bit is understanding what a worried person actually needs, then explaining the options in a way that lets them decide. Leila put it more simply. The clients she remembers are the ones who left less frightened than they arrived.
